name: Volatility Risk Premium (VRP) Harvesting
description: Implied volatility (what options market prices in) systematically exceeds realized volatility (what actually happens) by ~2-4 vol points on average. This 'volatility risk premium' exists because investors pay up for protection. The strategy sells options or variance swaps to systematically collect this premium.
core_logic: VRP = IV - RV (in vol points). Sell straddles/strangles or short VIX futures when VRP > threshold. Delta-hedge to isolate vol exposure. Exit when IV reverts toward RV.

sharpe_ratio.note: strong negative skew; crashes in vol spikes

risk.main_risk: Vega blowup in sudden volatility spikes (VIX > 40)

pros: Positive expected return from risk premium
cons: Negative skewness (small gains, rare catastrophic losses)
signal_buy: VRP < -2.0 vol points (IV unusually cheap → buy volatility)
signal_sell: VRP > +2.0 vol points (IV unusually expensive → sell volatility)
